text: Wax on, right hand. Wax off, left hand.
context: The iconic lesson that secretly teaches Daniel how to block while building muscle memory and patience.

text: Lesson not just karate only. Lesson for whole life. Whole life have a balance.
context: Miyagi explaining that the principles of karate and training apply to everything in life.

text: No such thing as bad student, only bad teacher.
context: Emphasizing the responsibility of the teacher and the unlimited potential in every student.

text: If come from inside you, always right one.
context: True power, correctness, and strength come from inner conviction rather than external approval.

text: Man who catch fly with chopstick, accomplish anything.
context: A seemingly impossible task that proves focus, patience, and belief can achieve the extraordinary.

text: Better learn balance. Balance is key. Balance good, karate good. Everything good. Balance bad, better pack up, go home.
context: Balance in stance, in mind, and in life is the foundation of success in karate and beyond.

text: Walk on road, hm? Walk right side, safe. Walk left side, safe. Walk middle, sooner or later get squish just like grape.
context: Half-measures and indecision lead to failure; full commitment to one path is required.

text: First learn stand, then learn fly. Nature rule, Daniel-San. Not mine.
context: Master the basics and fundamentals before attempting the advanced; respect the natural order of growth.
